title: The SpaceNews space unicorn tracker
summary: SpaceNews has published a tracker listing private space companies valued at $1 billion or more. This ongoing list highlights the growing number of 'space unicorns' in the industry.

title: Katalyst Space raises $12 million for GEO servicing demo mission
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies secured $12 million in funding for a geosynchronous orbit demonstration mission aimed at servicing satellites. The startup is preparing to launch a mission to boost the orbit of a NASA observatory.

title: PiLogic partners with Air Force lab to test satellite fault-prediction software
summary: PiLogic has partnered with an Air Force lab to test its satellite fault-prediction software, which uses probabilistic reasoning to diagnose spacecraft anomalies.

title: A UN agency that works for US space
summary: A UN agency, the International Telecommunication Union (ITU), is valued by U.S. policymakers for advancing American space standards and commercial interests despite broader skepticism toward the UN.

title: Geospatial industry launches maritime initiative
summary: The geospatial industry has launched a new maritime initiative to address growing demand from defense agencies and commercial shipping sectors for improved maritime domain awareness technologies.

title: Deep Space Network antenna mishap blamed on poor training and procedures
summary: A NASA investigation found that poor training and procedures caused millions of dollars in damage to one of its Deep Space Network antennas. The mishap highlights systemic issues in operational protocols.

title: Tianwen-2 makes series of burns on approach to asteroid, according to radio tracking
summary: China's Tianwen-2 spacecraft conducted multiple small propulsive maneuvers after a main burn on June 7, preparing for an asteroid rendezvous in July. The mission's progress was tracked via radio signals.

title: Gilat to buy Comtech satcoms business six years after failed merger
summary: Gilat Satellite Networks plans to acquire Comtech's space-related communications business to expand its defense capabilities, six years after a failed merger attempt. The deal marks a strategic move to strengthen Gilat's position in the satellite communications sector.

title: Space Force orders two more GPS satellites from Lockheed Martin for $514 million
summary: The U.S. Space Force has ordered two additional GPS satellites from Lockheed Martin for $514 million, bringing the total GPS 3F orders to 14 satellites. These satellites will feature anti-jam capabilities, digital payloads, and upgraded civilian navigation signals.

title: DARPA to explore ways to rapidly rebuild satellite networks if attacked
summary: DARPA is investigating methods to quickly restore satellite networks in the event of an attack, aiming to resume critical services within hours or weeks. The initiative seeks novel concepts for rapid network recovery.

title: The Nikon Z fc is our choice as the best budget camera — this retro-inspired beauty is at its best price of the year in this early Amazon Prime Day deal
summary: Nikon Z fc is highlighted as the best budget camera with a retro design, currently on sale during Amazon Prime Day. The mirrorless APS-C camera is praised for its looks and performance.

title: Watch a fireball burn a 300 mile path above the Midwest US (video)
summary: A fireball streaked across the Midwest US, traveling 300 miles in seconds before disintegrating. The event was captured on video, showcasing a spectacular display.

title: Don't miss the moon lead a dazzling mini 'planet parade' on June 17
summary: On June 17, the crescent moon will align with Jupiter, Venus, and Mercury in a mini 'planet parade' while passing through the Beehive open star cluster. This celestial event offers a rare opportunity to observe multiple planets and the moon in close proximity.

title: SpaceX IPO brings Starship to NYC | Space photo of the day for June 16, 2026
summary: SpaceX's IPO on Nasdaq coincides with Elon Musk becoming the world's first trillionaire. The event also highlights SpaceX's Starship program in NYC.

title: Move over 'Mass Effect': 'Exodus' just got an extended gameplay reveal, and we are beyond hyped for this sci-fi RPG
summary: A sci-fi RPG 'Exodus' has released an extended gameplay reveal, showcasing its immersive universe and gameplay. Players are invited to explore the Centauri Cluster and the planet Sang in this highly anticipated title.

title: Watch SpaceX launch 3 huge BlueBird direct-to-cell satellites from Florida early on June 17
summary: SpaceX is set to launch three AST SpaceMobile BlueBird direct-to-cell satellites using a Falcon 9 rocket from Florida on June 17. The event will be livestreamed.

title: James Webb Space Telescope forecasts extreme weather on exoplanet that rains rubies and sapphires
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) has provided detailed observations of an exoplanet with extreme weather conditions, including rain made of rubies and sapphires. This marks a significant advancement in exoplanet research using JWST's unparalleled observational capabilities.

title: SpaceX Dragon cargo capsule undocks from ISS, heads home to Earth
summary: A SpaceX Dragon cargo spacecraft undocked from the International Space Station on June 16 and is returning to Earth after a 20-hour journey.

title: Astrobotic unveils Griffin-1 lunar lander for NASA Moon Base mission
summary: Astrobotic unveiled its Griffin-1 lunar lander, designed to carry one of the heaviest payloads to the Moon for NASA's Moon Base mission.

title: SpaceX sends 24 Starlink satellites to orbit on 1st launch as a public company (video)
summary: SpaceX launched 24 Starlink satellites into orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base. This marked SpaceX's first launch as a public company.

title: NASA Uses Machine Learning to Enhance Flash Flood Warnings
summary: NASA has developed a machine learning system called TACLS to improve flash flood warnings by analyzing data from satellite networks. The system assists meteorologists at the National Weather Service in making more efficient flood forecasts.

title: Department of Health and Human Services Digital Stockpile & Manufacturing Response Network Challenge
summary: NASA's Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI) is assisting the Department of Health and Human Services in a crowdsourcing challenge for stockpile and manufacturing response. The NASA Tournament Lab will run the external challenge on behalf of the Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response (ASPR).

title: Metrics
summary: NASA has released its FY26 Services Catalog and Service Level Agreement (SLA) outlining service descriptions, chargeback rates, and service level indicators for NSSC services.

title: Aurora Australis
summary: A photograph taken from the International Space Station on June 5, 2026, captures the aurora australis over the Indian Ocean. The image highlights the colorful and dynamic display of particles and magnetism during an active solar event.

title: Nebraska’s Wide, Rolling Domain
summary: Nebraska's Sandhills cover about one-quarter of the state, making it the largest sand dune system in the Western Hemisphere.

title: Explore JPL to Take Place Oct. 10, 11
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) will host an open-house event called Explore JPL on October 10 and 11 to celebrate its 90th anniversary. Visitors can tour iconic facilities and explore thematic areas like Moon to Mars and Missions That Changed the World.

title: NASA’s Chandra Finds Unexpected Fireworks in Aftermath of Stellar Explosions
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory discovered unexpected changes in brightness in supernova remnants within the galaxy Messier 83. These remnants, typically fading slowly, exhibited dramatic variations, surprising astronomers.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from New Jersey Students
summary: NASA astronauts Chris Williams and Jessica Meir will answer STEM questions from New Jersey students during a live call from the International Space Station on June 18. The event will be streamed on NASA's YouTube channel.

title: NASA’s SpaceX CRS-34 Dragon Returns Packed with Space Station Science
summary: NASA's SpaceX CRS-34 Dragon spacecraft returned to Earth with a large payload of scientific samples and hardware from the International Space Station. The mission marked the 34th SpaceX commercial resupply mission for NASA.

title: Frontiers Forum Speaker Series
summary: NASA is hosting a public speaker series featuring their experts to discuss agency missions, aerospace technology, science, and innovation. The events will cover topics such as the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider had been preparing for the launch from Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. The progress follows Flight 12, which occurred about three weeks prior.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The country also initiated three missions for a single internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after a previous failure.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). These veteran astronauts will prepare for the upcoming crewed lunar landing mission.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: The upcoming week will see multiple space launches from China, Rocket Lab, and Starlink. Three Starlink missions, two from China, and one from Rocket Lab are scheduled.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communications. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent New Glenn rocket anomaly during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work ensures the success of ESA's missions by ensuring spacecraft components can survive in space.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This technology is opening new pathways for advancing space missions and education.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry. However, the lack of additional context makes it difficult to determine the exact details.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute test underwent a 'bake-out' to ensure its reliability. The procedure likely involved heating the parachute to remove contaminants or test its thermal resistance.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera raised £3 million to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance domestic technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with AFRL to develop AI anomaly detection technology, currently testing it on terrestrial flat sats with potential expansion into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a dip in stocks of other publicly traded space companies. Investors pulled back from major players in the sector following the event.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO will significantly boost the entire space industry by increasing investor confidence and liquidity. The move could shift the industry's reliance on private capital toward public markets.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about the type of economy that will emerge from this investment. The largest IPO in history could drive record capital into the sector.

title: What the SpaceX IPO Means for Europe’s Space Industry
summary: SpaceX's IPO is a significant event for the global space industry, with implications for Europe's space sector. The move could reshape competition and investment dynamics in the industry.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox has launched an update aimed at enhancing the effectiveness of AI agents in analyzing geospatial data. This update is designed to improve the capabilities of AI-driven geospatial analytics.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on the accuracy of SpaceX's $1.75T valuation ahead of its IPO, though they agree the space industry is experiencing significant growth.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC satellite, marking its entry into the orbital data center market. The Adagio-XL is designed to support data processing and storage in space.

title: Anuvu Names Former HBO Exec as CEO
summary: Anuvu appointed former HBO executive Simon Sutton as its new CEO, replacing longtime leader Josh Marks. The announcement was made on June 15.

title: Gilat to Acquire Comtech’s Satellite and Space Business, Targeting US Defense Growth
summary: Gilat Satellite Networks announced the acquisition of Comtech’s Satellite and Space Communications segment to strengthen its position in the U.S. defense market. The deal combines two leading companies in satellite ground technology.

title: Israel’s El Al Airline to Adopt Starlink for IFC
summary: Israel's El Al Airline will adopt SpaceX's Starlink for in-flight connectivity, with service gradually rolling out. The agreement marks a significant step in enhancing passenger connectivity on the national airline.

title: SpaceX Begins Trading at $150 Making Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire on Paper
summary: SpaceX started public trading on Nasdaq at $150 per share, an 11% increase over its IPO price, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is gaining critical importance as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services merge, becoming essential infrastructure for enterprises managing remote assets. The convergence highlights the growing necessity of reliable connectivity for fleet management, utilities, and industrial sites.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for improved disaster response and humanitarian aid. This initiative aims to leverage Starlink's satellite internet capabilities for global emergency preparedness.

title: Canadian Government Awards EO Satellite Contracts to Calian, Kepler, MDA Space
summary: The Canadian government awarded three Earth Observation (EO) satellite contracts worth C$2.4 million to Calian, Kepler, and MDA Space to enhance its EO capabilities.

title: Viasat Secures PTS-G U.S. Space Force SSC Swarm 1 Contract
summary: Viasat has secured a contract from the U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command to build, launch, and deliver the first satellite in a proliferated fleet as part of the PTS-G Swarm 1 mission. This contract marks a significant step in Viasat's expansion in the space industry.

title: SpaceX Set for IPO, Banking on Faith in Elon Musk and Massive AI Growth
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SPCX, marking its transition from a privately held company after 24 years. The move is driven by investor confidence in Elon Musk and expectations of significant growth in the AI sector.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has exited stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher network, a mobility infrastructure layer. The company aims to build a space-based network for transportation and logistics.
